data.title: Stock Market News Today, 1/30/26 – U.S. Stock Futures Fall after Microsoft Drags Down Nasdaq and S&P
data.description:
U.S. stock futures fell early Friday after a 10% share price drop in the tech giant Microsoft (MSFT) dragged down the Nasdaq and S&P 500. Futures on the Nasdaq 100 (NDX), the S&P 500 (SPX), and the Dow Jones Industrial Average (DJIA) were down 1.01%, 0.80%, and 0.76%, respectively, at 3:24 a.m. EST on January 30.

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

During Thursday's regular trading, the three major indexes closed mixed. The S&P 500 closed down 0.13%, the tech-focused Nasdaq Composite dropped 0.72%, and the Dow Jones Industrial Average gained 0.11%.

After the market closed, Apple (AAPL) shares jumped after exceeding fiscal first-quarter expectations, backed by robust iPhone sales. The company guided for revenue to grow between 13% and 16% year-over-year in Q2FY26. Moreover, SanDisk (SNDK) stock rallied 17% after beating Q2FY26 estimates and giving solid guidance.

Looking forward, investors will watch closely as President Donald Trump announces his pick to succeed Federal Reserve Chair Jerome Powell on Friday morning, heightening market uncertainty.

On the earnings front, results from SoFi Technologies (SOFI), ExxonMobil (XOM), Verizon (VZ), Chevron (CVX), and American Express (AXP) will be released today.

Notably, the U.S. 10-year Treasury yield was up, floating near 4.27%. WTI crude oil futures were trending lower, hovering near $64.14 per barrel as of the last check. Additionally, the Gold Spot U.S. dollar price decreased to nearly $5,154 per ounce on Friday, stalling a long-running rally.

Elsewhere, European indexes opened mixed today amid a flood of corporate earnings releases.

Asia-Pacific Markets Trade Lower

Asia-Pacific markets traded mostly lower on January 30, tracking Wall Street losses.

Hong Kong's Hang Seng Index fell 2.07%. In China, the Shanghai Composite declined 0.96%, while the Shenzhen Component lost 1.32%. Meanwhile, Japan's Nikkei slid 0.10%, but the Topix rose 0.59%.

Stay ahead of macro events with our up-to-the-minute Economic Calendar — filter by impact, country, and more.
data.author: Sheryl Sheth

data.title: AXP Earnings: American Express Stock Dips as Rising Costs Overshadow Dividend Boost
data.description:
American Express (AXP) shares faced pressure early Friday morning, dropping about 0.9% before the opening bell. While the company reported that transaction volume rose 9% to a record $445.1 billion, the excitement was dampened by a bottom-line miss. Amex earned $3.53 per share for the fourth quarter, falling just short of the $3.56 analysts had projected. To reward loyal shareholders, the board announced it will raise the quarterly dividend to 95 cents per share, a roughly 16% jump starting in the first quarter.

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

American Express' Platinum Card Overhaul Drives Up Expenses

The main reason for the profit squeeze was a surge in "customer engagement" costs. Chief Executive Officer Stephen Squeri explained that the company chose to invest heavily in a "strategic refresh" of its flagship Platinum Card and a brand-new mobile app experience. These updates include more than $3,500 in annual value through new dining, travel, and wellness credits. While these perks are designed to lock in high-spending Millennials and Gen Z users, the initial rollout costs and higher reward payouts ate into this quarter's earnings.

Record Spending Meets Political Headwinds

Despite the higher costs, Amex's core business remains strong, with net interest income rising 11% due to higher loan balances. However, the stock is also contending with outside political pressure. President Donald Trump has proposed a one-year 10% cap on credit card interest rates to combat the cost of living. Squeri warned in a Friday interview that such a cap would "upend the basic economics" of the industry and likely force banks to pull back on credit significantly.

AXP Outlook for 2026 Remains Cautiously Optimistic

Looking ahead, American Express issued full-year guidance for 2026, expecting earnings to land between $17.30 and $17.90 per share. The company is betting that its closed-loop network, where it acts as both the lender and the payment processor, will give it a safety net that traditional banks don't have. Management believes that by spending money now to refresh its premium products, they can continue to attract customers who spend nearly three times more than the average cardholder on other networks.

Is American Express a Good Stock to Buy?

On TipRanks, American Express stock (AXP) has a Moderate Buy consensus rating based on eight Buys, 11 Holds, and one Sell rating. The average 12-month AXP price target of $381.67 implies 6.5% upside potential from current levels.
data.author: Annika Masrani

data.title: Shareholders Sue Klarna Over False Statements in IPO Documents
data.description:
A class action lawsuit was filed against payments group Klarna (KLAR) on December 22, 2025.

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Plaintiffs – shareholders – in the federal securities class action allege that they acquired Klarna stock purchased or otherwise acquired Klarna securities pursuant and/or traceable to the Registration Statement issued in connection with Klarna's initial public offering conducted in September 2025. They are now seeking compensation for financial losses incurred upon public revelation of the company's alleged negligence during that time.

Investors who bought KLAR stock during that period can click here to learn about joining the lawsuit.

Klarna is a self-described global digital bank and flexible payments provider. It makes its AI-powered payments and commerce network available to 114 million active consumers. With operations in 26 countries, the company also says it helps more than 850,000 retail businesses "drive growth and loyalty through innovative payment and marketing solutions." Specifically, it does so by allowing consumers to pay with Klarna online, in-store, and through Apple (AAPL) Pay and Google (GOOGL) Pay.

Klarna has been in business since 2005, when three young entrepreneurs founded it as a business then known as Kreditor. Klarna adopted its current name in 2010 and acquired SOFORT in 2014. With that acquisition, the company says it became "Europe's fastest-growing online payments group." Just three years later, it obtained a full banking license, which allowed it to expand capabilities to serve its customers. The company says it reached another important milestone when its app launched in 2018.

Summary of Facts

The complaint alleges negligent preparation of the Registration Statement. As a result, it included untrue statements of important facts about the company's business, operational and financial results, but lacked corrective statements and legally required disclosures.

Specifically, the Registration Statement lacked truthful information about the risks of the company's loss reserves increasing substantially shortly after the IPO.

As a result, eight of the company's senior officers and/or directors, and its authorized representative in the United States – who all signed or authorized the signing of the Registration Statement – are named as Individual Defendants in the matter. Fourteen investment banking firms that acted as underwriters for the IPO are also named as Underwriter Defendants. All of these Defendants now stand accused of failing to conduct a "reasonable and diligent investigation" to ensure the veracity of the statements contained in the Registration Statement.

The truth, say the Plaintiffs, came out in several news articles published on November 18, 2025. Among other things, Bloomberg News reported that "[Klarna had] "posted a net loss of $95 million, as the firm set aside more money for potentially souring loans. [Klarna] said provisions represented 0.72% of gross merchandise volume, up from 0.44% a year ago."

A Closer Look

Shareholders allege that the Registration Statement contained untrue statements of important facts.

For example, the Prospectus (included in the Registration Statement), stated in relevant part: "Fair Financing allows consumers to pay for their purchase over a longer duration. We have designed our short-term credit products to serve a wide range of consumers, including those with varying credit histories and borrowing needs."

The Prospectus also stated: "For example, in 2019, we strategically decided to expand our operating model into additional geographies, with a particular focus on the United States, and in the following three years expanded into 12 additional markets. As part of that growth strategy, we recalibrated our risk model to reflect our higher risk appetite in those markets, which contributed to a rapid GMV growth and an increase in the number of consumers and merchants on our network but also led to higher credit losses […]"

Lastly, the Prospectus said: "The process of determining the allowance for credit losses is highly judgmental and subject to significant uncertainties. Future changes in economic conditions, consumer behavior or regulatory environment could necessitate adjustments to our allowance for credit losses."

The deadline to file for lead plaintiff in this class action is February 20, 2026.
data.author: David Craik

data.title: Apple Stock (AAPL) Slips in Pre-Market After Earnings— Analysts Weigh In on Outlook and AI
data.description:
Apple (AAPL) shares edged higher in after-hours trading on Thursday after the company reported fiscal first-quarter results that topped expectations. However, the stock slipped in early Friday pre-market trading, as investors continued to weigh Apple's outlook and its longer-term AI strategy.

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

For context, Apple reported earnings of $2.84 per share on revenue of $143.76 billion, beating Wall Street estimates. iPhone revenue came in at $85.3 billion, while Services reached $30 billion, marking another strong quarter for Apple's core businesses.

iPhone Sales and Outlook Draw Investor Attention

The iPhone remained Apple's main source of strength during the quarter. Sales were solid across regions, easing concerns that upgrade demand was fading. With iPhone revenue reaching $85.3 billion, investors saw that Apple's core business remains solid, even as growth comparisons are expected to get tougher later in the year.

Attention then shifted to Apple's outlook for the March quarter. The company said it expects revenue to grow between 13% and 16% year over year, which implies about $109 billion in sales at the midpoint. Apple also guided operating expenses to a range of $18.4 billion to $18.7 billion, pointing to steady cost control while it continues to invest.

The update pointed to steady demand after the holidays, supporting the stock's move higher.

Analysts Split on Apple's AI Position Post Q1

Following the Q1 earnings report, analysts differ on how Apple is positioned in artificial intelligence. For instance, a senior analyst at Seaport Research Partners warned that AI could become an "existential threat" for Apple if the company does not move fast enough. Speaking to Yahoo Finance, the analyst said rivals are moving quicker on AI tools and platforms, increasing pressure on Apple to show clearer progress.

Other analysts were less worried. They said Apple often moves at a slower pace when rolling out new tech, focusing on how it works with its existing products. Even so, many agree that AI is becoming more important for Apple and will play a bigger role in how investors view the stock.

What Investors Will Watch Next

Investor attention now turns to how Apple performs in the March quarter, when demand typically eases after the holiday period.

Apple's AI plans will also stay in focus, especially as rivals move faster with new features and products. How clearly Apple addresses that gap in the coming months could play a bigger role in where the stock trades from here.

Is Apple Stock a Buy? 

Turning to Wall Street, analysts have a Moderate Buy consensus rating on AAPL stock based on 19 Buys, 11 Holds, and two Sells assigned in the past three months, as indicated by the graphic below. Furthermore, the average AAPL price target of $298.84 per share implies 15.7% upside potential. However, it's worth noting that estimates will likely change following today's earnings report.
data.author: Shalu Saraf

data.title: Microsoft Stock (MSFT) Plunges 10% — But Here's What Crowd Wisdom Shows
data.description:
Microsoft (MSFT) shares slid more than 10% in Thursday's session despite better-than-expected earnings, marking the stock's worst one-day drop since March 2020. The stock is stabilizing in early Friday trading, but concerns around near-term cloud growth and rising AI spending remain in focus. Still, TipRanks' Crowd Wisdom data suggests many investors are treating the pullback as a buying opportunity rather than an exit signal.

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Market Digests Microsoft's Earnings

Microsoft reported adjusted earnings of $4.14 per share, beating Wall Street estimates of $3.91. Meanwhile, revenue rose 17% year over year to $81.27 billion, also topping expectations. At the same time, cloud demand remained strong, with commercial bookings up 230%, driven by large commitments from OpenAI and Anthropic, along with steady demand for Azure.

Even after the earnings beat, the stock sold off sharply. Investors appeared worried that demand for AI services is rising faster than Microsoft can supply it, limiting near-term revenue growth. Management also said capital spending will remain elevated as the company builds more data-center capacity, raising concerns about margins in the coming quarters.

What Does TipRanks' Crowd Wisdom Show?

Despite the sharp drop, broader investor behavior tells a different story. Microsoft holds a Positive investor sentiment, based on the activity of more than 851,000 investors tracked by TipRanks in the recent quarter.

TipRanks' Crowd Wisdom tool shows that 12% of all tracked portfolios currently hold Microsoft, with investors allocating an average of 9.34% of their portfolios to the stock. More notably, portfolio additions have continued, with holdings rising 0.5% over the past seven days and 2.4% over the past 30 days.
data.author: Shalu Saraf

data.title: 'Big Year for AWS,' Wedbush Reiterates Buy on Amazon Stock (AMZN) Ahead of Q4 Earnings
data.description:
Amazon (AMZN) is heading into its Q4 earnings report with strong support from Wall Street. Wedbush's five-star analyst Scott Devitt has reiterated his Buy rating on the stock and kept his $340 price target, implying roughly 40% upside. Devitt said 2026 could be a "big year for AWS," which could help push the stock toward his price target as the year unfolds. He also reaffirmed Amazon as Wedbush's top e-commerce pick for 2026.

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Overall, he is optimistic ahead of Amazon's earnings report, pointing to strong growth in AWS, steady performance in the core retail business, and robust advertiser demand.

Wedbush Stays Bullish on AMZN

Devitt noted that Amazon's cloud business, AWS, grew faster than expected, with a rising backlog and expanding capacity signaling continued strong demand. He also said investors are likely to focus on continued momentum in AWS and new AI opportunities, Amazon's progress toward higher profitability and margins, spending on infrastructure and AI, and ongoing growth in its advertising business.

According to Devitt,  Amazon has multiple ways to boost profits over time, including improving fulfillment efficiency and focusing more on higher-margin areas like AWS and advertising. He also pointed to potential growth drivers, including using automation and robotics in logistics, expanding AWS infrastructure, monetizing AI in Alexa and Rufus, generating revenue from Project Kuiper, and raising Prime subscription prices.

Wedbush Raises Q4 Estimates

Devitt and his team at Wedbush raised their estimates, forecasting Amazon's Q4 operating income at $25.2 billion, with an 11.8% margin—slightly above consensus. For the full year, analysts now expect operating income of $103 billion, with a 12.8% margin.

In Q3 2025, Amazon's operating income was $17.4 billion. Below is a screenshot showing Amazon's operating income across different business units.
data.author: Kirti Tak

data.title: SanDisk Stock Explodes 23% on Q2 Earnings Beat — Buy SNDK or Wait?
data.description:
SanDisk (SNDK) stock jumped sharply after the company delivered a standout Q2 earnings report and guidance, blowing past Wall Street estimates on both profit and revenue. Having said that, the stock's solid rally raises the risk of near-term volatility as some investors lock in profits. For long-term investors who believe AI-driven storage demand is still in the early innings, any pullbacks could present attractive entry points. Overall, SNDK remains compelling for patient investors—but chasing the stock immediately after a big spike may require caution.

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

SNDK stock jumped 17.17% in pre-market trading on Friday. Year-to-date, the shares are up more than 125%.

AI Boom Powers SanDisk Profit and Revenue Surge

SanDisk's fiscal second-quarter profit jumped 672% year-over-year to $803 million, or $5.15 per share. Meanwhile, revenue climbed 61% to $3.03 billion, beating analysts' $2.69 billion estimate.

The company's data-center revenue jumped 76% from a year earlier, as artificial intelligence continued to drive a sharp increase in demand.

What Lies Ahead for SanDisk?

Looking ahead, the company said it expects revenue of $4.4 billion to $4.8 billion for the next quarter, far above Wall Street's $2.9 billion estimate. Even the midpoint of the range implies nearly 60% upside versus expectations. SanDisk also guided for adjusted EPS of $12 to $14, compared with analysts' forecasts of just $5.11—a gap of more than 150%.

SanDisk's outlook underscores just how quickly AI-driven demand is accelerating, suggesting that earnings momentum is still building rather than peaking. Despite the strong quarter, management said the company was unable to fully meet surging demand and expects this supply-demand imbalance to persist into 2026. That level of visibility supports continued revenue growth and sustained pricing power.

At the same time, SanDisk's sharp improvement in profitability signals that operating leverage is kicking in as volumes rise.

What Is the Price Target for SanDisk?

Wall Street analysts currently rate SanDisk stock a Moderate Buy, with 10 Buy ratings and four Holds issued over the past three months. After the recent rally, the shares are now trading above SanDisk's average price target of $395.93, implying a downside of 26.6% from current levels.

That said, these ratings and targets are likely to change as analysts update their forecasts following the latest earnings report.
data.author: Kirti Tak

data.title: Roblox Stock (RBLX) Slides as Dutch Open Probe into Potential Violent and Sexual Imagery
data.description:
Shares in online gaming group Roblox (RBLX) were lower today as Dutch regulators opened a probe into whether it is exposing children to violent and sexual imagery.

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Potential Roblox Risks

The Netherlands Authority for Consumers and Markets (ACM) said it has launched an investigation into the gaming platform and would examine "potential risks to underage users in the EU." It said the probe would likely last around 12 months.

"The platform regularly makes the news, for example, due to concerns about violent or sexually explicit games that minors are exposed to," the ACM said in a statement.

Other concerns include reports of ill-intentioned adults targeting children on the platform and the use of misleading techniques to encourage purchases.

The ACM said it had also received reports of these allegations and "considers this sufficient reason to launch a formal investigation into possible violations of the rules by Roblox."

Under the EU's Digital Services Act (DSA), platforms must take "appropriate and proportionate measures" to ensure a high level of safety and privacy for minors.

The ACM said it could impose a "binding instruction, fine, or penalty" on Roblox if it finds that it has been breaking the rules.

The ACM certainly has teeth. In 2024 it fined Fortnite maker Epic Games €1.1 million because it ruled that vulnerable children had been exploited and pressured into making purchases in the game's Item Shop.

Roblox Responds

In response to the ACM probe, Roblox said that it was strongly committed to complying with the DSA rules and was looking forward to providing further clarity on its actions.

"We have invested significantly in building robust systems that align with the DSA's principles, including working to ensure the safety, security and privacy of minors," Roblox said.

It pointed out recent measures taken on the platform, such as introducing age check requirements for all users wishing to chat with others.
data.author: David Craik
